b7dab42f.name: Idea Benchmarking System (the tool)
b7dab42f.description: A CLI tool that benchmarks ideas across 12 domains using adaptive dimensions, a value formula, and a 5-phase workflow enforcing test + execution gates.

test.assumption: A 5-phase CLI workflow can enforce the principle across 12 domains without domain-specific code per idea.
test.test_method: Build it. Run test_run.py on 12 ideas across all domains. Verify: (1) no crashes, (2) correct scoring, (3) formula holds, (4) kill conditions fire, (5) value=0 for untested/unexecuted.
test.success_criteria: All 12 domains score correctly. Formula verified. No crashes. kill conditions fire on all domains. Serialization round-trips.
test.failure_criteria: Any domain crashes, any kill condition fails to fire, formula produces non-zero value for unexecuted ideas.
test.result_notes: 69/69 tests passed in the multi-domain stress suite. stress_test.py: all sections passed. Kill conditions verified. Formula verified. Round-trip verified.

b7dab42f.execution_notes: Built, tested, and running. 6 scripts. 12 domains. 60+ stress tests.

22a4db9c.name: The IdeaValue Formula  (GroupA × GroupB × T × E)
22a4db9c.description: IdeaValue = (d1×d2×d3) × (d4+d5+d6) × test_passed × executed. Any zero multiplier collapses the total to zero. This encodes the principle mathematically.

test.assumption: The multiplicative structure correctly assigns zero value to any idea that is untested OR unexecuted, regardless of benchmark score.
test.test_method: Algebraic proof + exhaustive case testing: if test_passed=0, formula=0; if executed=0, formula=0; if any GroupA dimension=0, formula=0; if GroupB=0, formula=0.
test.success_criteria: All 4 zero-collapse cases confirmed. No case produces non-zero value when any multiplier is zero.
test.failure_criteria: Any single case where untested or unexecuted idea produces value > 0.
test.result_notes: Stress test Section 2: all 9 boundary condition tests passed. Formula behaves algebraically correctly.

22a4db9c.execution_notes: Implemented in models.py:idea_value(). Verified by stress_test.py Section 2 and 6.

8c87193c.name: Domain-Adaptive Dimension Profiles
8c87193c.description: Instead of one fixed set of 6 dimensions, each domain carries its own complete dimension profile — same structure, different meaning per domain. A math conjecture and a business idea should not be scored on the same questions.

test.assumption: 12 independent dimension profiles, each with 6 dimensions and 3 score descriptions each, correctly differentiate idea types without collapsing to a generic set.
test.test_method: Automated integrity checks: (1) all 12 domains have exactly 6 dimensions, (2) all dimensions have scores 0/1/2, (3) kill_dim exists in every domain's dimensions, (4) no score description is blank.
test.success_criteria: All integrity checks pass for all 12 domains.
test.failure_criteria: Any domain fails any integrity check.
test.result_notes: stress_test.py Section 1: 11/11 integrity checks passed. All 12 domains verified.

8c87193c.execution_notes: Implemented in domains.py. 12 domain profiles. Verified by stress_test.py.

b1a319c6.name: Kill Condition Mechanism  (load-bearing dimensions)
b1a319c6.description: Each domain has one dimension that is so foundational that scoring zero on it makes further benchmarking meaningless. The system stops immediately on a kill-dimension zero — not because of a policy, but because the logical basis for the idea collapses.

test.assumption: Setting the kill dimension to 0 while all others = 2 causes the benchmarking system to stop and flag the idea as killed, regardless of other dimension scores.
test.test_method: stress_test.py Section 5: for each of 12 domains, set all scores=2 except kill_dim=0. Verify formula behavior. phase_benchmark() interactive verification against kill_dim logic.
test.success_criteria: Kill condition triggers correctly for all 12 domains. No crash.
test.failure_criteria: Any domain passes kill-dim=0 without triggering.
test.result_notes: 12/12 domains verified. Kill conditions trigger correctly. Formula collapses to 0 when kill_dim is in GroupA. Interactive phase_benchmark() tested manually.

b1a319c6.execution_notes: Implemented in benchmark.py:phase_benchmark(). Verified by stress_test.py Section 5.

99f18ff0.name: Falsifiability as a Universal Dimension
99f18ff0.description: Every domain, regardless of how different its ideas are, must include a dimension that asks: can this be proven wrong? A mathematical conjecture without a provability path, a creative concept with no audience metric, a policy with no impact KPI — all share the same flaw: they cannot be tested. Falsifiability is therefore domain-invariant.

test.assumption: Requiring a falsifiability-type dimension in all 12 domains does not produce absurd or inapplicable questions for any domain.
test.test_method: Review all 12 domain falsifiability dimensions. Ask: (1) Is the question domain-appropriate? (2) Does it genuinely test whether the idea can be disproven? (3) Is scoring 0 on it a meaningful signal?
test.success_criteria: All 12 falsifiability dimensions are domain-appropriate and score-0 is a meaningful signal in every case.
test.failure_criteria: Any domain has a falsifiability dimension where score=0 does not meaningfully signal an untestable idea.
test.result_notes: Reviewed all 12. Mathematics uses 'Provability Path'. Creative uses 'Audience Testability'. Law uses 'Measurable Impact'. All are appropriate. One weakness: Creative's falsifiability dimension is less rigorous than Science's. Accepted — the domain demands it.

99f18ff0.execution_notes: Implemented across all 12 domains in domains.py. Each domain has a falsifiability-type dimension.

be2323d0.name: Core Principle: Ideas Worth Zero Until Tested + Executed
be2323d0.description: The foundational claim the entire system rests on: an untested idea is indistinguishable from a delusion. An unexecuted idea, even if tested, is still hypothetical. Only tested-positive AND executed ideas have demonstrated value. This is not motivational. It is a logical claim.

test.assumption: The multiplicative formula correctly encodes the logical claim: if either test_passed=0 OR executed=0, value=0, regardless of idea quality.
test.test_method: (1) Algebraic proof: show formula collapses to 0 when either multiplier=0. (2) Empirical: run 12 ideas with various states through the formula. (3) Counterexample search: find any idea where value>0 without test or execution.
test.success_criteria: No counterexample found. Formula algebraically verified. 12 ideas confirm empirically.
test.failure_criteria: Any idea produces value>0 without passing test or without execution.
test.result_notes: Algebraically proven. Empirically confirmed across all 12 domains. Counterargument about intrinsic value noted — treated as out-of-scope for this system's definition of value.

be2323d0.execution_notes: The principle is the foundation of the entire system. Encoded in models.py:idea_value(). Enforced by benchmark.py:phase_record_result().

a5d4880b.name: Recursive Weighted Pivots
a5d4880b.description: Recursive Weighted Pivot logic: NewScore = (1-w)*OldScore + w*InputScore. Allows granular confidence building as research deepens, preventing knee-jerk score changes from single pieces of evidence.

test.assumption: The weighted average formula correctly accumulates evidence over time and handles boundary weights (0.0 and 1.0) correctly.
test.test_method: Manual trace of 5 consecutive pivots with varying weights. Verify final score matches expected algebraic result.
test.success_criteria: Algebraic match across all 5 steps. w=1.0 completely overwrites. w=0.0 preserves old score.
test.result_notes: Verified manually and via stress_test.py. Formula is robust.

6d9694c4.name: Automated Knowledge Status Tracking
6d9694c4.description: Automated progression through 4 stages (UNRESEARCHED, EXPLORING, KNOWLEDGEABLE, EXPERT) based on objective metrics: research word count, test results, and execution status.

test.assumption: Knowledge status correctly reflects the objective state of the idea without manual overrides.
test.test_method: Create 4 ideas in each state. Verify `knowledge_status` property returns correct enum value for each.
test.success_criteria: 100% accuracy in status reporting across all 4 stages.
test.failure_criteria: Any idea showing incorrect status (e.g. EXPERT when not executed).
test.result_notes: Property logic in models.py verified. Status updates are deterministic.

6d9694c4.execution_notes: Implemented as a property in models.py:Idea.knowledge_status.

8a9d222c.name: JSON Robustness & Error Recovery
8a9d222c.description: Graceful handling of corrupted or malformed JSON data by returning an empty dataset and warning instead of crashing. Protects the user from losing access to the tool due to a single bad write.

test.assumption: The system will not crash if ideas.json is truncated or contains garbage.
test.test_method: stress_test.py Section 3: write random text to ideas.json, then call load_all().
test.success_criteria: No crash. Warning printed to stderr. Returns empty dict.
test.result_notes: Stress test Section 3 passed. Robustness confirmed.

8a9d222c.execution_notes: Implemented in models.py:load_all() with try/except block.

0181d412.name: Multi-Domain Stress Suite (69 tests)
0181d412.description: A comprehensive 69-test suite covering integrity, formula boundaries, kill conditions, and performance across 13 domains. Ensures system integrity after every modification.

test.assumption: The stress test suite catches regressions across all 12+ domains and formula boundary conditions.
test.failure_criteria: Any failing test in the suite.
test.result_notes: All tests pass. Suite covers all major system components.

f44d7918.name: Deep Research Domain
f44d7918.description: A specialized domain for high-complexity, multi-stage investigation into recurring problems or novel fields. Features tailored dimensions for methodological rigor and investigator fit.

test.assumption: The Deep Research dimensions correctly capture the complexity of long-term inquiry compared to the standard Project or Science domains.
test.test_method: Manual review of domain questions. Run a test research initiative through the benchmark.
test.success_criteria: Dimensions are distinct and provide meaningful differentiation for high-complexity tasks.
test.failure_criteria: Dimensions feel redundant with Science or Project domains.
test.result_notes: Verified by applying to the system's own development history. Provides much better resolution for research-heavy tasks.
